title: The Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde
author: Robert Louis Stevenson

setting.note: Stevenson keeps the geography deliberately shadowy, but the districts are real. Hyde's sinister side-door opens on a by-street near Soho; the same building is Dr Jekyll's handsome house around the corner in a West End square; Dr Lanyon holds court in Cavendish Square, the citadel of medicine; Hyde keeps rooms in Soho; and Sir Danvers Carew is beaten to death by night in a lane near the river at Westminster. The named districts are exact; the exact doors are composite, which is what the labels are for.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Robert Louis Stevenson, first published 1886; the author died in 1894.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States (published 1886) and in the United Kingdom and the EU, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1894).
rights.attribution: Robert Louis Stevenson, The Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde (1886). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The lurid brown of the London fog that reinvades Soho like a nightmare, taken straight from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.
